name: ChatGPT Deep Research (GPT 5.2)

strengths.notes: CONFIRMED across 2 projects with actual output review. R migration review was the most thorough of the three — 7 major sections with Assessment → Risks → Recommendations → Priority structure. Caught issues others missed.

Found Python 3.13 not available in Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (rocker/r-ver base), which would have been a deployment dead-end. Also caught SIGTERM vs KeyboardInterrupt mismatch, mixed-tick reads without atomic fencing. Neither Gemini nor DeepSeek would have found these.
R review cited Railway docs, SQLite docs, Rocker Project docs, Docker best practices. Not just opinions — researched claims with sources. This is unique among the three reviewers.

Identified the overarching concern that wasn't in the prompt: 'new reliability coupling — bot and UI share container lifecycle, must decide which failures take down whole container vs self-heal.' This is the kind of systems-level thinking that justifies the routing decision.

web_ui_input.staging_strategy: file — write to staging dir, user drags into upload zone (paperclip icon)
web_ui_input.notes: User confirmed file upload works. long_documents: false may limit total context — need to test limits with 22-file manifest.

output_characteristics.format: multi-section, each with Assessment → Specific Risks → Concrete Recommendations → Priority
output_characteristics.actionability: very high — config knobs, PRAGMA settings, code patterns specified
output_characteristics.research_depth: deep — cites official docs and community reports
output_characteristics.style: thorough systems engineer — finds failure modes, proposes mitigations with priority levels

context_preferences.notes: Needs full system internals to reason about failure modes and architectural coupling

pattern_stability: high — same role, same quality across app architecture and deployment infrastructure
